title: Edward Fane's Rosebud (From "Twice Told Tales")
summaries: "Edward Fane's Rosebud (From 'Twice Told Tales')" by Nathaniel Hawthorne is a short story first published in Twice-Told Tales, a collection that appeared in two volumes in 1837 and 1842. The story was among those previously published anonymously in magazines and annuals before being collected. Twice-Told Tales gained recognition for Hawthorne's originality and pure style, with critics praising his depth of feeling and genius, though some found his reliance on allegory excessive.
